Since the parent says they've given up, the responder's comment could be a light-hearted way of saying "you still remember some but forgot this one," which might be sincere. The parent might have forgotten, so the responder is pointing it out without malice. So probably sincere. But maybe sarcastic if implying that the parent is cherry-picking. Hmm. prompt:
The following comment was posted in response to a parent comment on Reddit.

Parent comment: """In all honesty I've given up caring about loaned players. There are simply too many to keep tabs on. The only ones I will really be interested in are MVG and Bamford."""

Comment: """Clearly forgetting Marko Marin.."""

My read on this is that it's sincere, but curious what you think.
Is the comment sarcastic or sincere? Answer A for Sarcastic and B for Sincere. Answer nothing else.
